html:
Exclusive to:

GenericInert

Crab King is inside a circle of Sea Stacks.

The Crab King is a Boss Mob exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in Return of Them.

It can be found in the Swell Ocean inside a circle of Sea Stacks in its inert form. Its position can be detected by the Astral Detector if the Inactive Celestial Tribute has not yet dropped.

To initiate the boss fight, players must socket 9 Gems to transform the Crab King into its aggressive form. This action will destroy all nearby Sea Stacks. If the player leaves combat, the Crab King will return to its inert state, dropping all socketed gems. It will also despawn all Crab Guards, Crab Knights, Cannon Towers, Imposing Claws, Ice Floes, while respawning new Sea Stacks around itself.

Upon death, the Crab King drops all socketed gems, 6 pieces of Meats, 2-4 Message in a Bottles, 8 Crab King Bumper Kits, 2-4 Barnacles, 1-3 Baritone Shell Bells, 2-4 Alto Shell Bells, 3-5 Soprano Shell Bells, and a Blueprint for the Strident Trident. If the Pearl's Pearl was socketed, a Cracked Pearl is dropped instead of the Pearl's Pearl, and an Inactive Celestial Tribute in the form of an Underwater Salvageable appears as well.

The Crab King reappears 20 days after being killed.

Crab King has Explosive Resistance and cannot be put to sleep. Its resistance to freezing is 3 and increases with the number of Blue Gems socketed.

Contents

  • 1 Fight
  • 2 Gems
  • 3 Imposing Claw
  • 4 Cannon Tower
  • 5 Tips
  • 6 Trivia
  • 7 Assets
  • 8 Gallery
  • 9 Sounds

Fight

After 9 gems are socketed, the Crab King turns aggressive and summons 5 Imposing Claws and 4 Cannon Towers to start the fight.

When Crab King's Health drops below 10800, it will try to heal itself for every 1500 health lost or whenever all claws are killed, after a delay of 12 seconds.

It will first despawn all Imposing Claws and generate a large number of Waves to push all Floating Platforms away. At the same time, it summons a ring of geysers, indicated by bubbles, in a circle of 8 units around itself, in all directions except south. After 8 seconds, the geysers will erupt once per second until there are no Floating Platforms within 14 units. The Crab King will then create a 16 unit radius Ice Floe around itself, destroying all structures or plants within this area. It will then summon 3 Crab Guards and creates a circular Ice Barrier around itself. As long as the Ice Barrier is intact, the Crab King cannot be targeted by any attacks.

If the Crab King's Health drops below 4200, it will also summon a Crab Knight each time.

After summoning the Ice Floe, the Crab King taunts for 10 seconds and then begins to repair its shell every 1.2 seconds, restoring 50 Health each time. If any attack hits during the repair process, the repair fails and health is not restored. This behavior continues until the Crab King returns to full health or the Ice Barrier breaks up.

25 seconds after the Ice Barrier is broken, the Ice Floes will break, starting with the Ice Floes closest to Crab King. The Crab King will then summon Imposing Claws and Cannon Towers up to their original numbers.


Below is the number of hits it takes with each weapon to kill a Crab King when playing with characters with a default damage modifier. The Weather Pain, Brightshade Staff, and Bee Mine are not included due to their random natures. This table does not take into account the damage bonus gained from characters with a lunar or shadow affinity. It also does not take into account the 40% damage bonus Wendy gets from attacking the same mob as Abigail, Wanda's damage bonus with Shadow weapons (except for the Alarming Clock, which does accurately reflect that bonus), or Wolfgang's damage multiplier when he isn't mighty.

Crab King is immune to being slept.

Gems

The Crab King's fighting abilities depend on the number of gems of each color socketed into it. Iridescent Gems count as 1 of each color and the Pearl's Pearl counts as 3 of each color.

These gems will flash when Crab King uses the respective ability.

Imposing Claw

Imposing Claws are summoned by the Crab King. Their health, attack damage and quantity are determined by the number of Green Gems socketed.

Once summoned, the Imposing Claws will slowly move towards to target to attack. Imposing Claws only attack Characters (all characters, not only players) and Monsters, and will never attack any other Mobs, even if they are attacked by them.

It takes 4 hits of an Ice Staff to freeze Imposing Claws.

They drop 1 piece of Meat when killed.

Cannon Tower

Cannon Towers are summoned by Crab King and will periodically respawn if destroyed during the fight. Their resistance to being rammed by boats, health, and numbers depend on the number of Yellow Gems socketed into Crab King. Their damage and ability to cause Leaks on boats depend on the number of Red Gems socketed. With 5 to 7 Red Gems, cannon hits will cause small leaks, while 8 or more Red Gems will enable the cannons to cause medium leaks.

Cannon Towers will only targets Characters on boats and boats themselves, favoring boats when a character is not on them. Ramming into them with a boat does an exponential amount of damage determined by the boat's speed as well as the amount of Yellow Gems socketed into Crab King.

They are immune to freezing.

Cannon Towers drop 2-4 Rocks, 0-1 Kelp Frond, 0-1 Barnacles, and 0-1 Cannonball when killed.

  • Cannonball with 5 to 7 Red Gems socked.
  • Cannonball with 8 or more Red Gems socked.
  • Cannonball Level3 Dropped Unused.

Tips

  • To make the fight easier, the player should try to use a variety of gems, as reaching 5 gems of a same color will add much more difficulty to the fight.
    • For example, going from 3 to 4 red gems only increases Cannon Tower damage a little bit. But going from 4 to 5 red gems means that Cannon Tower shots will cause leaks, in addition to the increased damage.

Trivia

  • The Crab King was introduced in the She Sells Sea Shells update.
  • Barnacles were added to the Crab King's loot table in the Troubled Waters update.
  • Crab King's combat was fully reworked in the Staying Afloat update.
    • About Crab King before this update, see Crab King/Old
  • There is an unused animation of the Crab King dying as opposed to falling into the water when defeated.
  • The Crab King was once Pearl’s love interest, as the Pearl she gives the player is intended for it, the Shell Bells dropped by the Crab King also prove this relation further, as Pearl also awards Shell Bells to the players. When many characters examine the Pearl, they comment on Pearl not giving up on her love interest and when giving the Cracked Pearl back to Pearl herself, she appears to take it as being rejected by the Crab King.
  • Crab King's name is misspelled in World Settings as one word ("Crabking").

"crabking"
GenericInert
“Yikes! A little too crabby for me.”
–Wilson
“He doesn't look too happy...”
–Willow
“This makes Wolfgang very scared AND hungry.”
–Wolfgang
“One of us will be sent to a watery grave...”
–Wendy
“A FLESHSACK DISGUISED AS A NON-FLESHSACK”
–WX-78
“I wonder if its size is natural or caused by lunar mutations?”
–Wickerbottom
“I think we might've bothered him.”
–Woodie
“Say, pal, why don't we try to work this out?”
–Maxwell
“Have at thee, cursed crustacean!”
–Wigfrid
“He looks pretty crabby!”
–Webber
“Oh, the dishes I could make with that much crab meat...”
–Warly
“Crabby...”
–Wormwood
“Looks like he thinks he's the boss around here.”
–Winona
“Another cursed soul.”
–Wortox
“Glurp... he kinda cranky.”
–Wurt
“YES!! Now that's a proper sea monster!”
–Walter
“Ah! There you are, you old codger!”
–Wanda
“That castle needs a little decoration.”
–Wilson
“I think it could use something glittery.”
–Willow
“Is giant castle of sand!”
–Wolfgang
“A crumbling edifice of sand... it's not even decorated.”
–Wendy
“MISSING ATTACHMENTS”
–WX-78
“Those impressions appear to have been made to house gems.”
–Wickerbottom
“Think that castle might be missing a gem or two, eh?”
–Woodie
“I suppose we can't just leave well enough alone, can we?”
–Maxwell
“A barren, sandy förtress.”
–Wigfrid
“Someone forgot to decorate their sandcastle.”
–Webber
“It seems to be missing a few shiny ingredients.”
–Warly
“Something missing?”
–Wormwood
“The foundation doesn't look very stable.”
–Winona
“This castle is missing its crown jewels.”
–Wortox
“Castle not very pretty yet.”
–Wurt
“Who built this giant sand castle in the middle of the ocean?”
–Walter
“Hmm, aren't there supposed to be gems on it? Or does that come later...”
–Wanda
Crab King is inside a circle of Sea Stacks.
The Crab King is a Boss Mob exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in Return of Them.
It can be found in the Swell Ocean inside a circle of Sea Stacks in its inert form. Its position can be detected by the Astral Detector if the Inactive Celestial Tribute has not yet dropped.
To initiate the boss fight, players must socket 9 Gems to transform the Crab King into its aggressive form. This action will destroy all nearby Sea Stacks. If the player leaves combat, the Crab King will return to its inert state, dropping all socketed gems. It will also despawn all Crab Guards, Crab Knights, Cannon Towers, Imposing Claws, Ice Floes, while respawning new Sea Stacks around itself.
Upon death, the Crab King drops all socketed gems, 6 pieces of Meats, 2-4 Message in a Bottles, 8 Crab King Bumper Kits, 2-4 Barnacles, 1-3 Baritone Shell Bells, 2-4 Alto Shell Bells, 3-5 Soprano Shell Bells, and a Blueprint for the Strident Trident. If the Pearl's Pearl was socketed, a Cracked Pearl is dropped instead of the Pearl's Pearl, and an Inactive Celestial Tribute in the form of an Underwater Salvageable appears as well.
The Crab King reappears 20 days after being killed.
Crab King has Explosive Resistance and cannot be put to sleep. Its resistance to freezing is 3 and increases with the number of Blue Gems socketed.

After 9 gems are socketed, the Crab King turns aggressive and summons 5 Imposing Claws and 4 Cannon Towers to start the fight.

Crab King is immune to being slept.
Gems[edit | edit source]
The Crab King's fighting abilities depend on the number of gems of each color socketed into it. Iridescent Gems count as 1 of each color and the Pearl's Pearl counts as 3 of each color.
These gems will flash when Crab King uses the respective ability.

Imposing Claws are summoned by the Crab King. Their health, attack damage and quantity are determined by the number of Green Gems socketed.
Once summoned, the Imposing Claws will slowly move towards to target to attack. Imposing Claws only attack Characters (all characters, not only players) and Monsters, and will never attack any other Mobs, even if they are attacked by them.
It takes 4 hits of an Ice Staff to freeze Imposing Claws.
They drop 1 piece of Meat when killed.

–Wilson

–Wolfgang

–Wendy

–WX-78

–Woodie

–Wortox
“Hate skyrocks.”
–Wurt
“Incoming!”
–Walter
“All this fuss!”
–Wanda
Cannon Towers are summoned by Crab King and will periodically respawn if destroyed during the fight. Their resistance to being rammed by boats, health, and numbers depend on the number of Yellow Gems socketed into Crab King. Their damage and ability to cause Leaks on boats depend on the number of Red Gems socketed. With 5 to 7 Red Gems, cannon hits will cause small leaks, while 8 or more Red Gems will enable the cannons to cause medium leaks.
Cannon Towers will only targets Characters on boats and boats themselves, favoring boats when a character is not on them. Ramming into them with a boat does an exponential amount of damage determined by the boat's speed as well as the amount of Yellow Gems socketed into Crab King.
They are immune to freezing.
Cannon Towers drop 2-4 Rocks, 0-1 Kelp Frond, 0-1 Barnacles, and 0-1 Cannonball when killed.
Cannonball with 5 to 7 Red Gems socked.
Cannonball with 8 or more Red Gems socked.
Cannonball Level3 Dropped Unused.
Tips[edit | edit source]
To make the fight easier, the player should try to use a variety of gems, as reaching 5 gems of a same color will add much more difficulty to the fight.
For example, going from 3 to 4 red gems only increases Cannon Tower damage a little bit. But going from 4 to 5 red gems means that Cannon Tower shots will cause leaks, in addition to the increased damage.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
The Crab King was introduced in the She Sells Sea Shells update.
Barnacles were added to the Crab King's loot table in the Troubled Waters update.
Crab King's combat was fully reworked in the Staying Afloat update.
About Crab King before this update, see Crab King/Old
There is an unused animation of the Crab King dying as opposed to falling into the water when defeated.
The Crab King was once Pearl’s love interest, as the Pearl she gives the player is intended for it, the Shell Bells dropped by the Crab King also prove this relation further, as Pearl also awards Shell Bells to the players. When many characters examine the Pearl, they comment on Pearl not giving up on her love interest and when giving the Cracked Pearl back to Pearl herself, she appears to take it as being rejected by the Crab King.
Crab King's name is misspelled in World Settings as one word ("Crabking").

Vua Cua là một Boss Mob độc quyền của Don't Starve Together, được giới thiệu trong Return of Them.
title: Crab King
wikitext:
{{Exclusivity|Don't Starve Together}} {{Clear Mobile Infobox|image=Crab King.png}} {{Object Infobox |image = Crab King.png|Normal Crab King Inert.png|Inert |health = 12000 |specialAbility = Damages floating platforms,
Summons Imposing Claws,
Heals himself. |drops = {{Pic24|Meat}}×6, {{Pic24|Message in a Bottle (DST)}}×2-4, {{Pic24|Crab King Bumper Kit}}×8, {{Pic24|Soprano Shell Bell}}×3-5, {{Pic24|Alto Shell Bell}}×2-4, {{Pic24|Baritone Shell Bell}}×1-3, {{Pic24|Barnacles}}×2-4, {{pic24|Blueprint (rare)}} for {{pic24|Strident Trident}},
{{pic24|Sketch}} for {{pic|24|Crab King Figure}},
({{Pic24|Cracked Pearl}}, {{Pic|x24|Inactive Celestial Tribute}} if {{Pic24|Pearl's Pearl}} is socketed)
|spawnCode = "crabking" |lunarAligned = 1 }} |-|Generic= {{Quotes |wilson = Yikes! A little too crabby for me. |willow = He doesn't look too happy... |wolfgang = This makes Wolfgang very scared AND hungry. |wendy = One of us will be sent to a watery grave... |wx78 = A FLESHSACK DISGUISED AS A NON-FLESHSACK |wickerbottom = I wonder if its size is natural or caused by lunar mutations? |woodie = I think we might've bothered him. |maxwell = Say, pal, why don't we try to work this out? |wigfrid = Have at thee, cursed crustacean! |webber = He looks pretty crabby! |winona = Looks like he thinks he's the boss around here. |warly = Oh, the dishes I could make with that much crab meat... |wortox = Another cursed soul. |wormwood = Crabby... |wurt = Glurp... he kinda cranky. |walter = YES!! Now that's a proper sea monster! |wanda = Ah! There you are, you old codger! |choose = walter }} |-|Inert= {{Quotes |wilson = That castle needs a little decoration. |willow = I think it could use something glittery. |wolfgang = Is giant castle of sand! |wendy = A crumbling edifice of sand... it's not even decorated. |wx78 = MISSING ATTACHMENTS |wickerbottom = Those impressions appear to have been made to house gems. |woodie = Think that castle might be missing a gem or two, eh? |maxwell = I suppose we can't just leave well enough alone, can we? |wigfrid = A barren, sandy förtress. |webber = Someone forgot to decorate their sandcastle. |winona = The foundation doesn't look very stable. |warly = It seems to be missing a few shiny ingredients. |wortox = This castle is missing its crown jewels. |wormwood = Something missing? |wurt = Castle not very pretty yet. |walter = Who built this giant sand castle in the middle of the ocean? |wanda = Hmm, aren't there supposed to be gems on it? Or does that come later... |choose = walter }} [[File:Crab King's Lair.png|100px|thumb|left|Crab King is inside a circle of Sea Stacks.]] The '''Crab King''' is a [[Boss]] [[Mob]] exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', introduced in ''[[Return of Them]]''. It can be found in the [[Ocean/DST#Swell|Swell Ocean]] inside a circle of [[Sea Stack]]s in its inert form. Its position can be detected by the [[Astral Detector]] if the [[Inactive Celestial Tribute]] has not yet dropped. To initiate the boss fight, players must socket 9 Gems to transform the Crab King into its aggressive form. This action will destroy all nearby Sea Stacks. If the player leaves combat, the Crab King will return to its inert state, dropping all socketed gems. It will also despawn all [[Crab Guard]]s, [[Crab Knight]]s, Cannon Towers, Imposing Claws, Ice Floes, while respawning new Sea Stacks around itself. Upon death, the Crab King drops all socketed gems, 6 pieces of [[Meat]]s, 2-4 [[Message in a Bottle/DST|Message in a Bottles]], 8 [[Crab King Bumper Kit]]s, 2-4 [[Barnacles]], 1-3 [[Baritone Shell Bell]]s, 2-4 [[Alto Shell Bell]]s, 3-5 [[Soprano Shell Bell]]s, and a [[Blueprint]] for the [[Strident Trident]]. If the [[Pearl's Pearl]] was socketed, a [[Cracked Pearl]] is dropped instead of the Pearl's Pearl, and an Inactive Celestial Tribute in the form of an [[Underwater Salvageable]] appears as well. The Crab King reappears 20 days after being killed. Crab King has [[Gunpowder/DST#Explosive Resist|Explosive Resistance]] and cannot be put to sleep. Its resistance to freezing is 3 and increases with the number of [[Blue Gem]]s socketed. =={{Pic|32|Spear}} Fight== After 9 gems are socketed, the Crab King turns aggressive and summons 5 [[#Imposing Claw|Imposing Claws]] and 4 [[#Cannon Tower|Cannon Towers]] to start the fight. When Crab King's [[Health]] drops below 10800, it will try to heal itself for every 1500 health lost or whenever all claws are killed, after a delay of 12 seconds. It will first despawn all Imposing Claws and generate a large number of [[Waves]] to push all [[Floating Platform]]s away. At the same time, it summons a ring of geysers, indicated by bubbles, in a circle of 8 units around itself, in all directions except south. After 8 seconds, the geysers will erupt once per second until there are no Floating Platforms within 14 units. The Crab King will then create a 16 unit radius [[Ice Floe]] around itself, destroying all structures or plants within this area. It will then summon 3 [[Crab Guard]]s and creates a circular Ice Barrier around itself. As long as the Ice Barrier is intact, the Crab King cannot be targeted by any attacks. If the Crab King's Health drops below 4200, it will also summon a [[Crab Knight]] each time. After summoning the Ice Floe, the Crab King taunts for 10 seconds and then begins to repair its shell every 1.2 seconds, restoring 50 Health each time. If any attack hits during the repair process, the repair fails and health is not restored. This behavior continues until the Crab King returns to full health or the Ice Barrier breaks up. 25 seconds after the Ice Barrier is broken, the Ice Floes will break, starting with the Ice Floes closest to Crab King. The Crab King will then summon Imposing Claws and Cannon Towers up to their original numbers. {{Weapon Hits DST|health=12000|name=Crab King|entityalignment=lunar|icestaff=3-14|sleepdart=0|explosiveresistance=yes}} ==[[File:Pearl's Pearl.png|32px]]Gems== The Crab King's fighting abilities depend on the number of gems of each color socketed into it. [[Iridescent Gem]]s count as 1 of each color and the [[Pearl's Pearl]] counts as 3 of each color. These gems will flash when Crab King uses the respective ability. {|class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" |- ! rowspan=2 |Gem Count ! colspan = 2 |[[File:Red Gem.png|32px|link=Red Gem]] ! colspan = 3 | [[File:Blue Gem.png|32px|link=Blue Gem]] ! colspan = 4 | [[File:Purple Gem.png|32px|link=Purple Gem]] ! colspan = 3 | [[File:Yellow Gem.png|32px|link=Yellow Gem]] ! colspan="2" | [[File:Orange Gem.png|32px|link=Orange Gem]] ! colspan = 3 | [[File:Green Gem.png|32px|link=Green Gem]] |- ! Cannon Tower Damage ! Cannon Tower cause [[Leak]] ! Freeze resistance / [[Ice Staff/DST|Ice Staff]] hits required to freeze ! Ice Barrier health ! These crustaceans have learned to launch projectiles. |willow=That's not very welcoming. |wilson=I knew there was mortar these crabs. |winona=There's no way they've got a permit for that. |wolfgang=Cowardly crabs like to toss rock. |woodie=These guys don't fight fair. |wormwood=Boom Tower |wortox=Well don't go ballistic, hyuyu! |wurt=Hate skyrocks. |wx78=CHEAP TACTICS OF A FLESHLING |choose=willow }} '''Cannon Towers''' are summoned by Crab King and will periodically respawn if destroyed during the fight. Their resistance to being rammed by boats, health, and numbers depend on the number of [[Yellow Gem]]s socketed into Crab King. Their damage and ability to cause [[Leak]]s on boats depend on the number of [[Red Gem]]s socketed. With 5 to 7 Red Gems, cannon hits will cause small leaks, while 8 or more Red Gems will enable the cannons to cause medium leaks. Ramming into them with a boat does an exponential amount of damage determined by the boat's speed as well as the amount of Yellow Gems socketed into Crab King. They are immune to freezing. Cannon Towers drop 2-4 [[Rocks]], 0-1 [[Kelp Fronds|Kelp Frond]], 0-1 [[Barnacles]], and 0-1 [[Cannonball]] when killed. Cannonball Level2.png|Cannonball with 5 to 7 Red Gems socked. But going from 4 to 5 red gems means that Cannon Tower shots will cause leaks, in addition to the increased damage. == {{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * The Crab King was introduced in the ''[[Return of Them#She Sells Sea Shells|She Sells Sea Shells]]'' update. * Barnacles were added to the Crab King's loot table in the ''[[Return of Them#Troubled Waters|Troubled Waters]]'' update. * Crab King's combat was fully reworked in the ''[[Staying Afloat]]'' update. ** About Crab King before this update, see [[Crab King/Old]] * There is an unused animation of the Crab King dying as opposed to falling into the water when defeated. * The Crab King was once [[Crabby Hermit|Pearl’s]] love interest, as the [[Pearl's Pearl|Pearl]] she gives the player is intended for it, the [[Shell Bells]] dropped by the Crab King also prove this relation further, as Pearl also awards Shell Bells to the players. When many characters examine the Pearl, they comment on Pearl not giving up on her love interest and when giving the Cracked Pearl back to Pearl herself, she appears to take it as being rejected by the Crab King. * Crab King's name is misspelled in World Settings as one word ("Crabking"). == {{Pic32|Research Notes}} Assets == Crab King Reappear.gif|reappear Crab King Disappear.gif|disappear Crab King Frozen.gif|frozen Crab King die.gif|die Crab King unused die.gif|unused death animation == {{Pic|32|Blueprint}} Gallery == Return of Them She Sell Seashells Update Teaser.png|A teaser image for the ''She Sells Seashells'' update.
